programs.programName: Medicaid Buy-In for Children (MBIC) / Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), Qualifying Individual (QI)

verified.notes: MBIC is for children age 18 and younger with disabilities; no senior (55+) pathway. QMB/SLMB/QI are Medicare Savings Programs for seniors 65+ but separate from MBIC; query combines unrelated programs. No 2026 senior-specific data found for combined programs.

programs.programName: Texas State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)

verified.notes: Texas SHIP is also known as HICAP (Health Information, Counseling, and Advocacy Program). Search results confirm the program provides free counseling to Medicare-eligible individuals and their families, but do not contain specific eligibility criteria such as age, income limits, or asset limits. The phone number is from the official SHIP locator. For complete eligibility details, contact the program directly or consult official Texas state health insurance resources.

programs.notes: Texas SHIP is also known as HICAP (Health Information, Counseling, and Advocacy Program). The phone number is from the official SHIP locator. For complete eligibility details, contact the program directly or consult official Texas state health insurance resources.

verified.notes: CRITICAL: CBA waiver was TERMINATED on 10/31/2014 per federal Medicaid records. The program no longer exists as of 2026. Search result [3] from medicaid.gov explicitly states 'WAIVER TERMINATED on 10/31/2014.' Age requirement (65+) and phone number extracted from historical program documentation [1], but this program is no longer operational. No current asset or income limits available because program is defunct. Families should contact Texas HHSC for current alternatives such as HCS, TxHmL, CLASS, DBMD, or MDCP waivers, or Community First Choice (CFC).
